name: Las Merchanas - El Colmenar Circular Trail
name_original: Yacimiento Castro Vetón de Lumbrales (Las Merchanas)
description: Hike the moderate 9.1-mile Las Merchanas - El Colmenar Circular Trail in Arribes del Duero Natural Park, exploring ancient history.
region_intro: Hiking around Lumbrales, situated in the western part of Salamanca, Spain, provides access to diverse natural landscapes. The region is defined by deep river canyons carved by the Duero, Camaces, and Huebra rivers, particularly within the Arribes del Duero Natural Park. Expansive dehesa landscapes, characterized by oak woodlands and pastures, also contribute to the varied terrain. These features offer a range of environments for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Lumbrales The most popular hiking route is Viewpoint Peña de la Vela – Natural Park of Las Arribes del Duero, a 4.1 miles (6.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 48 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views of the Duero River and the surrounding natural park. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mirador de Saucelle – Natural Park of the Arribes del Duero, a moderate 7.2 miles (11.6 km) path. This trail features impressive views of the Duero River and the Saucelle reservoir. Local hikers also love the Settlement Castro Vetón of Lumbrales (Las Merchanas), a 9.1 miles (14.7 km) trail leading through a privileged natural setting and an ancient archaeological site, often completed in about 3 hours 49 minutes. Hiking around Lumbrales is defined by river canyons, dehesa landscapes, and numerous viewpoints overlooking the Duero River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more demanding treks. The routes in Lumbrales are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 3 reviews. More than 40 hikers have used komoot to explore Lumbrales's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Lumbrales?
A: There are 10 hiking trails around Lumbrales, offering a variety of experiences. These include 4 easy routes and 6 moderate routes, ensuring options for different fitness levels.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I encounter on hikes near Lumbrales?
A: Hiking around Lumbrales offers diverse landscapes, primarily within the Arribes del Duero Natural Park. You'll experience dramatic river canyons carved by the Duero, Camaces, and Huebra rivers, as well as expansive dehesa landscapes characterized by oak woodlands and pastures. These features provide unique ecosystems and scenic views.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, there are several easy trails. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993141448/viewpoint-pena-de-la-vela-natural-park-of-las-arribes-del-duero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint Peña de la Vela – Natural Park of Las Arribes del Duero&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.7 km route that takes approximately 1 hour 48 minutes, offering beautiful views of the Duero River.

Q: Can I find trails that offer impressive views of the river canyons?
A: Absolutely. The region is renowned for its spectacular river canyons. Many trails lead to viewpoints (miradores) overlooking the Duero River and its gorges. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993135689/mirador-de-saucelle-natural-park-of-the-arribes-del-duero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Saucelle – Natural Park of the Arribes del Duero&lt;/a&gt;, which provides impressive views of the Duero River and the Saucelle reservoir.

Q: Are there any historical or archaeological sites accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, the area is rich in history. You can hike to the significant pre-Roman Vettonian settlement of Castro de Las Merchanas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993351580/settlement-castro-veton-of-lumbrales-las-merchanas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Settlement Castro Vetón of Lumbrales (Las Merchanas)&lt;/a&gt; trail, for example, leads through a privileged natural setting to this ancient archaeological site.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest to see around Lumbrales?
A: Beyond the trails themselves, Lumbrales offers several points of interest. You can visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4321091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of San Felices de los Gallegos&lt;/a&gt;, explore the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5335377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vetón Hillfort of Lumbrales (Las Merchanas)&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy panoramic views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de la Vela Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334730" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saucelle Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in the area?
A: Yes, there are circular routes that allow you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23740433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Fraile y la Monja – Castro Vetón de El Castillo loop from Saldeana&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 4.4 km loop.

Q: Is Lumbrales a good destination for family-friendly hikes?
A: Yes, Lumbrales offers several easy routes that are suitable for families. These trails often feature gentle terrain and offer engaging natural scenery, making them enjoyable for all ages. The shorter, less strenuous paths are ideal for family outings.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Lumbrales?
A: The hiking routes in Lumbrales are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Hikers often praise the stunning natural scenery, particularly the dramatic river canyons and the tranquility of the dehesa landscapes, as well as the historical points of interest along the trails.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Lumbrales?
A: The best time to hike in Lumbrales is typically during spring or autumn. In these seasons, the weather is milder, and the natural surroundings are particularly lush and green, enhancing the scenic beauty of the river valleys and dehesa landscapes. Summers can be hot, and winters may be cooler and wetter.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the historic 'Camino de Hierro' (Iron Way)?
A: While not explicitly listed in the current routes, the historic 'Camino de Hierro' is a significant attraction in the region. This 19th-century railway line, with its tunnels and bridges, offers unique hiking and cycling experiences. You may find local information or specific routes that incorporate sections of this path for a distinct historical adventure.

Q: Is it possible to reach hiking trailheads using public transport?
A: Public transport options directly to all trailheads in the rural area around Lumbrales may be limited. It is generally advisable to have your own transport for greater flexibility. However, local bus services might connect Lumbrales to nearby towns, from which some trailheads could be accessible, though this would require checking specific schedules and routes.

Q: Are there specific parking areas for hikers near the trails?
A: For many popular viewpoints and trail starting points, you will typically find designated parking areas. Given the region's focus on outdoor tourism, especially within the Arribes del Duero Natural Park, efforts are usually made to provide access for visitors. It's always a good idea to check specific trailhead information for parking details.
tips:
The province of Salamanca is well known for being Vetón territory since it has two of the most important deposits in the area, Yecla la Vieja and Las Merchanas.

The sites dating back to pre-Roman times up to the most recent ones related to the culture of the castros and boars are known as Vetón territory.
It has been studied that the occupation of these territories dates back to the Iron Age 500 years ago and even much earlier up to the 5th century in the case of this particular site of Las Merchanas. In the golden age of these territories, these settlements were mainly populated by shepherds and warriors who, according to various Roman writers, were known as "vetones".

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Las Merchanas - El Colmenar Circular Trail?
faqs.answer: This moderate hike usually takes around 3 hours and 50 minutes to complete. However, your actual time may vary depending on your pace, stops for photos, and exploration of points of interest.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this trail is varied. You can expect a mix of paths, some of which may be stony or difficult in sections. While the overall elevation gain is modest, some parts might feel more challenging due to the ground underfoot. It's best to wear sturdy hiking shoes.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or features can I see on the trail?
faqs.answer: A significant point of interest along the route is the Vetón Hillfort of Lumbrales (Las Merchanas). This historical site offers a glimpse into ancient settlements and provides a unique cultural element to your hike.

faqs.question: Is the Las Merchanas - El Colmenar Circular Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Rated as 'moderate', this trail is generally suitable for hikers with a reasonable level of fitness. While not overly strenuous in terms of elevation, the varied terrain means it's not ideal for absolute beginners. Some sections may require sure-footedness.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this trail is typically during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, and some initial stretches might have thistles, making long trousers advisable. Winter can be pleasant, but always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this hike?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for the Las Merchanas - El Colmenar Circular Trail are not provided, but trails in the Arribes del Duero Natural Park often have designated parking areas near villages or key access points. It's advisable to check local signage or community forums for the most current parking information near Lumbrales or El Colmenar.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Las Merchanas - El Colmenar Circular Trail?
faqs.answer: While specific rules for this exact trail are not detailed, generally, dogs are permitted on trails within the Arribes del Duero Natural Park. However, they should always be kept on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety and comfort of other hikers. Please clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike in the Arribes del Duero Natural Park?
faqs.answer: As a natural park, Arribes del Duero generally does not require specific permits or charge entrance fees for hiking on its public trails. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of protected areas and private property. There might be specific rules for certain activities or areas, so it's good practice to check the park's official website if you plan activities beyond hiking.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife and plants might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The Arribes del Duero region is known for its diverse Mediterranean flora and fauna. Along the trail, you might spot various birds of prey, and if you're lucky, some larger mammals like deer or wild boar. The plant life includes cork oaks, pines, ferns, and mossy rocks, creating a rich natural environment.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near the village of Lumbrales. While specific facilities directly at the trailhead are not guaranteed, you can typically find cafes, restaurants, and public restrooms within the village itself. It's a good idea to plan for these stops before or after your hike.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this route?
faqs.answer: For this moderate trail, essential items include sturdy hiking shoes, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and appropriate clothing for the weather. A map or GPS device is also recommended, especially if you plan to explore beyond the main path. Don't forget your camera for the scenic views!
